message:
🐛 fix: eliminate 6 GA4 auth errors on /alerts page (#9957) (#9962)

Root cause: two separate issues combining to produce 6 'No authentication
token available' GA4 errors on the /alerts page.

Issue 1 — redundant useUniversalStats() call in Alerts.tsx
DashboardPage already calls useUniversalStats() internally and merges
any custom getStatValue getter with it. Alerts.tsx was also calling
useUniversalStats() and pre-merging before passing to DashboardPage,
causing every sub-hook (useOperators, useOperatorSubscriptions,
useIngresses, …) to run twice and emit double the auth errors.
Fix: remove the redundant call; pass getDashboardStatValue directly.

Issue 2 — missing token guards in three hooks
- useOperators: when no JWT token exists, SSE is skipped (sseAvailable
  is false) but the code fell through to api.get('/api/gitops/operators'),
  which emits an auth GA4 event before throwing.
- useOperatorSubscriptions: same pattern.
- useIngresses: no token check before api.get(LOCAL_AGENT_HTTP_URL+
  '/ingresses'), which also fires the auth check in api.get().

Fix for all three: return early when no token is available instead of
reaching the api.get() call. The hooks already handle demo mode and
will show empty/cached state until auth is established.

Fixes #9957

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
